findings.title: E2E uses anchored full-string regex; misses combo format chip and couples to label shape
findings.why_it_matters: Active format chip copy is built as `Format: ${filters.format.join(', ')}` in LibraryFilters. A single-format audiobook filter renders as exactly `Format: audiobook`, so the regex matches the regression this test targets. However, a buggy state that still auto-includes audiobook alongside another value (e.g. `audiobook` + `epub`) would render `Format: audiobook, epub`, which does not match `/^Format:\s*audiobook$/i`; `toHaveCount(0)` would still pass, yielding a false negative. The assertion also breaks if copy changes (Oxford comma, localized labels, or separator) even when behavior is correct.
findings.suggested_fix: Assert absence of the format chip using a stable selector, e.g. `expect(page.getByTestId('library-active-filter-remove-format')).toHaveCount(0)`, optionally plus a store/fixture assertion that `filters.format` is unset after navigation. If keeping text, use a broader pattern (e.g. `/Format:.*audiobook/i`) only together with an assertion that no format chip exists at all.
evidence: LibraryFilters builds chip label via join(', ') for multi-value filters
Anchored /^Format:\s*audiobook$/i does not match 'Format: audiobook, epub'

findings.title: New Library format-default and filtering logic lacks unit or component tests
findings.why_it_matters: The diff changes `modeBooksForMedia`, `activeModeLabel`, and a gated `useEffect` (sessionStorage `libraryFormatCleared`, `prevFilterFormatRef`, mixed vs single-modality libraries, empty-library reset). Only one E2E checks mixed-library no auto chip; branches for single-format auto-apply, user clearing the chip, library emptied reset, and 'Items' empty-state buttons are not covered by new tests. Regressions in those branches would slip past CI unless caught elsewhere.
findings.suggested_fix: Extract the format-default decision into a pure helper (inputs: books, current format, flags) and unit-test cases: mixed modalities leave format unset; audiobook-only applies ['audiobook']; ebook-only applies ['epub','pdf']; user clear persists session flag; empty books resets refs. Alternatively add focused RTL tests around Library with a stubbed book store.

findings.title: BookCard status pill tests only exercise the EPUB card layout, not the audiobook layout
findings.why_it_matters: `makeBook` defaults to `format: 'epub'`. The audiobook branch uses different markup (`book.status !== 'unread'` before rendering BookStatusBadge). Reading/Finished tests never render the audiobook path, so spacing/regression in the square-cover status row is unproven.
findings.suggested_fix: Duplicate Reading/Finished (and unread absence) cases with `format: 'audiobook'` and ids distinct from defaults so queries stay unambiguous.

findings.title: BookStatusBadge unread early-return has no direct unit test
findings.why_it_matters: The new guard is behavior on BookStatusBadge itself; BookCard tests cover only one consumer layout. BookListItem and any future callers could diverge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add BookStatusBadge.test.tsx: unread → container empty/null; reading/finished render expected labels (lightweight snapshot or text).

residual_risks.description: Mixed-format E2E depends on seed data containing both an EPUB-like and an audiobook entry; if TEST_BOOKS or seed shape changes to a single modality, the test could pass while production mixed libraries regress.

Timer- or store-order flakes: Library mount effect vs browse tab paint could theoretically race; the current assertion is only on eventual chip absence, which is usually stable but not exercised for flakiness in burn-in.

testing_gaps.description: No automated test for single-modality library still auto-applying audiobook-only or ebook-only format on first mount.
No test for user clearing an active format filter (non-empty → empty) setting sessionStorage and not re-applying default on remount within session.
No test for empty mixed-format view: activeModeLabel 'Items', copy, and data-testid buttons library-format-empty-view-audiobooks / library-format-empty-view-ebooks.
E2E mixed-format case does not assert positive outcome (e.g. both formats visible in browse) — only refutes exact audiobook-only chip string.
